rationale: The frozen candidate is a production EmbeddedKnowledge Lesson Format v1/schema-v3 pack for PREM-LPP-002 version 0.1.3 at c97a4516a7b40820ae434ed73cbe31e68582e38a, mapped to the declared standard-risk graph outcome and prerequisite. The pull-request head differs from that candidate only by the three named v3 review artifacts. Each counted artifact validates structurally, approves this exact lesson identity, version, and 40-character candidate, and has an exact fenced GitHub Comment-review transport authored by github:VSBDev against the same candidate commit; the founding-stage policy accepts that transport when the disclosed operator is also the pull-request author. The eligible set supplies one academic, one learning-design, and one accessibility-and-rights approval through three unique run IDs, three declared providers and model families (Anthropic/Claude, Google/Gemini, and OpenAI/GPT), and one accountable principal, satisfying standard-lesson-v2. None of the counted reviews contains a major or blocking finding. The current academic record verifies the vocabulary crosswalk, source support, graph placement, classifications, and deterministic 25-point mastery gate. The learning-design record verifies objective alignment, fading support, and genuine instrument-redesign transfer. The accessibility-and-rights record verifies equivalent response routes, the repaired production assessment reader, semantic/reflow/print behavior, source and asset rights, privacy, security, and provenance, while retaining explicit limits on learner testing, non-web exports, and assistive-technology coverage. The two current minor findings remain useful future refinements but do not make the taught model inaccurate, remove mastery evidence, or deny an equivalent learning path: the crosswalk already states that fragile can route through either A or P, and the worked example supplies complete reasoning before a self-explanation prompt followed by retrieval and feedback. Historical artifacts target earlier candidates and are excluded from quorum; their former major and blocking issues are resolved in the frozen candidate or shared reader verified by the fresh v3 reviews. Provenance is disclosed and attested, not independently verified. With quorum recomputed and no credible unresolved blocker, merge is warranted subject to the bounded governance promotion and final checks below.
dissent: The academic reviewer records a minor terminology risk: the crosswalk does not explicitly say that the syllabus's informal phrase 'recall is fragile' within Acquiring is not identical to this lesson's operational fragile node state, which may route through either Acquiring or Practising. Preserve this as a future clarity revision; changing the crosswalk now would stale all current approvals.

The learning-design reviewer records a minor feedback gap: the worked example's final Birch-versus-Copper self-explanation prompt does not immediately reveal an explicit expected explanation or verification check. Preserve this as a future instructional refinement; changing the scene now would stale all current approvals.
conditions: The maintainer may make one post-candidate governance promotion that changes only lesson.json status from draft to published, lesson.json sourceConfidence from pending-review to medium-high, and every retained claims.json reviewStatus from pending-review to reviewed; no teaching, assessment, evidence, rights, attribution, authorship, review, or adjudication text may change under this authorization.

After the bounded governance promotion, regenerate only deterministic site output and rerun npm run validate, the relevant test suite, pull-request readiness, and GitHub-provenance checks. The three counted review transports and this adjudication transport must remain exact, eligible, non-dismissed, and pinned to the frozen candidate.

Any attempt to resolve the recorded dissent or otherwise change substantive pack content requires a new candidate commit and fresh role quorum plus fresh adjudication; it is not authorized as part of this merge decision.
